3.happening: Start the reachable frontier at index 0.
3.why: You begin standing on the first index, so index 0 is always reachable.
3.invariant: Every index from 0 to farthest is reachable from the start.

4.why: The cursor will scan every index from 0 to n-1.
4.invariant: n is the total number of indices.

5.happening: Advance the cursor to index i = {i}.
5.why: The greedy scan folds in one index at a time, left to right.
5.invariant: Every index left of the cursor has already extended the frontier.

6.happening: Index i = {i} sits beyond the frontier ({farthest}) — it is unreachable.
6.why: No earlier jump reached this far, so nothing from here on can be reached either.
6.invariant: If the cursor passes the frontier, the last index is impossible to reach.

6.happening: Index i = {i} is within the frontier ({farthest}) — we can stand here.
6.why: Some earlier jump reaches at least this far, so this index is reachable.
6.invariant: Every index up to farthest is reachable.

7.happening: The cursor escaped the reachable frontier — return false.
7.why: The last index lies past a gap no jump can cross.

8.happening: From index {i} you can jump up to nums[{i}] = {nums[i]}, landing as far as index {i + nums[i]}.
8.why: reach is the farthest index a single jump from i can touch.

9.happening: Extend the frontier to the farther of {farthest} and the new reach.
9.why: The frontier must always hold the greatest index reachable by any jump seen so far.

10.happening: The cursor crossed the whole array without ever escaping the frontier — return true.
10.why: Every index along the way was reachable, so the last index is too.

init.why: You start standing on the first index before any jump.

loop.why: Greedy extends the reachable frontier one index at a time.
loop.invariant: Indices left of the cursor are already folded in.

check.happening: Testing whether the cursor is still within the reachable frontier.
check.why: If the cursor has passed the frontier, the end can't be reached.
check.invariant: An index is reachable exactly when it is ≤ farthest.

update.happening: Extending the frontier using the jump available at index i.
update.why: A jump from index i can reach as far as i + nums[i].
update.invariant: farthest is the greatest reachable index so far.

return.happening: Reporting whether the last index is reachable.
return.why: A single left-to-right pass settles the question.
return.invariant: The answer reflects whether the cursor ever escaped the frontier.
